sourceMovie.title: Tommy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tommy works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and music blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pink Floyd: The Wall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tommy clicked for you, Pink Floyd: The Wall (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and music, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Dirt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tommy clicked for you, The Dirt (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and music, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tommy?
faq.answer: Try Pink Floyd: The Wall, The Dirt, Better Man—each shares DNA with Tommy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tommy?
faq.answer: Tommy (1975) is best described as Drama, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tommy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
